page: Amazon Web Services g4dn.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 vCPUs (4 physical cores) running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance is equipped with a single NVIDIA T4 GPU from the Turing architecture, providing 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is provided via a 225 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 10 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance for general CPU and memory operations, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU tests falling into the middle 50% tier. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by lower-tier results in single-threaded gzip and bzip3 compression tests. However, the hardware excels in GPU-accelerated tasks. Large language model (LLM) inference benchmarks for a 7B parameter model show top-tier performance (top 10%) in both prompt processing and text generation, demonstrating the efficiency of the NVIDIA T4 accelerator for smaller-scale deep learning tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g4dn.2xlarge offers a balanced entry point for GPU compute without the premium cost of high-end accelerators. The combination of dedicated Intel Xeon cores, DDR4 memory, and local NVMe storage makes it suitable for workloads such as machine learning inference, small-scale model training, video transcoding, and graphics-intensive applications.
description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a single NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. It includes a 225 GB local NVMe SSD and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. While general CPU benchmarks and single-core performance tests place the instance in the average to weak categories, its GPU-accelerated capabilities are highly competitive. Specifically, LLM inference benchmarks for 7B parameter models achieve top-tier performance in both prompt processing and text generation. This hardware profile makes the instance a cost-effective choice for machine learning inference, graphics-intensive rendering, and video transcoding workloads where dedicated GPU acceleration is required but massive CPU compute is not the primary bottleneck.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, a 16 GB NVIDIA T4 GPU, and 225 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for machine learning inference and graphics workloads.

tagline: An x86_64 instance featuring dedicated NVIDIA T4 GPU acceleration and local NVMe storage for machine learning inference and graphics workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 vCPUs and 32 GB DDR4 RAM
NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB VRAM for hardware-accelerated workloads
225 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in 7B LLM inference prompt processing and text generation
Suited for machine learning inference, video transcoding, and graphics rendering
